title: Pimenta yumana
intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called "Pimenta yumana." However, I can provide information about a plant called "Pimenta dioica" or "Pimenta officinalis," commonly known as "Allspice." 

Pimenta dioica is a tropical evergreen tree native to Central and South America, and the Caribbean, with its leaves, bark, and fruit being used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Pimenta dioica include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Pimenta dioica include essential oils, such as eugenol, caryophyllene, and limonene, as well as phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Pimenta dioica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to relieve digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems.
